title: What Is a Terminal?
subtitle: A text-based way to talk to your computer

sections.content: A **terminal** is a text-based interface where you type commands to interact with your computer. Instead of clicking icons and menus, you type what you want to do.
sections.analogy: Think of your computer as a restaurant. The graphical interface (Finder, Desktop) is like dining in — you look at the menu, point at what you want, a waiter brings it. The terminal is like calling the kitchen directly — you tell the chef exactly what you want using specific words. Same food, different way of ordering.
keyPoints: The terminal does the same things as your graphical interface, just with typing instead of clicking
Developers use the terminal because: it's faster for many tasks, some tools only work in the terminal, and you can automate repetitive tasks
On Mac: it's called Terminal.app (built-in). On Windows: PowerShell or Windows Terminal
The `$` symbol in a terminal is the prompt — it means the terminal is waiting for you to type something

sections.instruction: Watch how a terminal works. These three commands show where you are, what's in your current folder, and how to move into another folder.

lines.annotation: Shows where you are — 'print working directory'
Lists what's in the current folder — 'list'
Moves into the projects folder — 'change directory'

sections.question: What is a terminal?
options: A text-based interface where you type commands to interact with your computer
A special app for watching videos
An external device you plug into your computer
sections.explanation: The terminal is simply a different way to interact with your computer — through text commands instead of clicking. Everything you can do by clicking, you can do by typing a command.

sections.question: What does the $ sign in a terminal mean?
options: It means the command costs money
It's the prompt — the terminal is waiting for you to type a command
It's a shortcut for the home folder
sections.explanation: The $ is called the 'prompt'. It's the terminal saying 'I'm ready — what do you want me to do?' You type your command after the $, then press Enter.

sections.question: Why do developers prefer using the terminal?
options: Because graphical interfaces don't work for coding
Because the terminal is faster for many tasks, and some tools only work in the terminal
Because the terminal is easier to learn than clicking
Because the terminal uses less memory
sections.explanation: The terminal is powerful because it's fast once you know the commands, some development tools only work in the terminal, and you can combine commands and automate tasks. In the next level, you'll experience this firsthand.

completionMessage: You now know what a terminal is and why developers use it. In the next level, you'll open a terminal and type real commands. Don't worry — we'll start simple and build up from there.

milestone.title: Computers Are Not Magic
summary: Files are containers for information, each with a name and type
Folders organize files and can be nested inside each other
Paths are addresses that tell you exactly where a file is
File extensions tell the computer what kind of file it is
Programs are files with instructions the computer runs
The terminal is a text-based way to control your computer
milestone.nextLevelTeaser: Next up: Level 1 — Your First 30 Minutes in the Terminal. You'll type real commands and build things. See you there!
